\begin{document}

\chapter{Theorems}

\begin{theorem}[Pythagorean Theorem]
For a right triangle with legs $a$ and $b$ and hypotenuse $c$,
\begin{equation}
a^2 + b^2 = c^2
\end{equation}
\end{theorem}

\begin{definition}
A \textbf{vector space} is a set $V$ with addition and scalar multiplication.
\end{definition}

\begin{lemma}
Every finite group has a trivial subgroup.
\end{lemma}

\begin{proof}
The identity element forms a trivial subgroup. \qed
\end{proof}

\end{document}
